response:
Here are comprehensive pytest unit tests for the `slugify` function:

```python
import pytest
from your_module import slugify  # Replace with actual import


class TestSlugify:
    """Test suite for slugify function."""

    # Happy path tests
    def test_basic_conversion(self):
        """Test basic text to slug conversion."""
        assert slugify("Hello World") == "hello-world"
        assert slugify("Python Programming") == "python-programming"
        assert slugify("Test Slugify Function") == "test-slugify-function"

    def test_lowercase_conversion(self):
        """Test that text is converted to lowercase."""
        assert slugify("UPPERCASE TEXT") == "uppercase-text"
        assert slugify("MiXeD CaSe TeXt") == "mixed-case-text"
        assert slugify("Title Case Example") == "title-case-example"

    def test_special_characters_removal(self):
        """Test removal of special characters."""
        assert slugify("Hello!@#$%^&*()World") == "helloworld"
        assert slugify("Test@Email.com") == "testemailcom"
        assert slugify("Product #123") == "product-123"
        assert slugify("Price: $19.99") == "price-1999"

    def test_whitespace_handling(self):
        """Test handling of various whitespace characters."""
        assert slugify("  Hello  World  ") == "hello-world"
        assert slugify("Hello\tWorld") == "hello-world"
        assert slugify("Hello\nWorld") == "hello-world"
        assert slugify("Hello   World") == "hello-world"

    def test_underscore_conversion(self):
        """Test conversion of underscores to hyphens."""
        assert slugify("hello_world") == "hello-world"
        assert slugify("test__underscore") == "test-underscore"
        assert slugify("_leading_underscore_") == "leading-underscore"

    def test_multiple_hyphens_consolidation(self):
        """Test consolidation of multiple consecutive hyphens."""
        assert slugify("Hello--World") == "hello-world"
        assert slugify("Test---Multiple---Hyphens") == "test-multiple-hyphens"
        assert slugify("Hello---") == "hello"

    def test_edge_hyphens_removal(self):
        """Test removal of hyphens at the beginning and end."""
        assert slugify("-Hello World-") == "hello-world"
        assert slugify("---Test---") == "test"
        assert slugify("-") == ""
        assert slugify("--") == ""

    # Edge cases
    def test_empty_string(self):
        """Test handling of empty string."""
        assert slugify("") == ""
        assert slugify("   ") == ""
        assert slugify("\t\n") == ""

    def test_single_word(self):
        """Test single word input."""
        assert slugify("Hello") == "hello"
        assert slugify("  Python  ") == "python"
        assert slugify("TEST") == "test"

    def test_numbers_only(self):
        """Test input containing only numbers."""
        assert slugify("123") == "123"
        assert slugify(" 456 ") == "456"
        assert slugify("1 2 3") == "1-2-3"

    def test_special_characters_only(self):
        """Test input containing only special characters."""
        assert slugify("!@#$%^&*()") == ""
        assert slugify("---") == ""
        assert slugify("_ _") == ""

    def test_mixed_alphanumeric(self):
        """Test mixed alphanumeric input."""
        assert slugify("Python3.9") == "python39"
        assert slugify("Version 2.0 Release") == "version-20-release"
        assert slugify("API v1.2.3") == "api-v123"

    def test_international_characters(self):
        """Test handling of international characters."""
        # Note: Non-ASCII characters will be removed by [^\w\s-] pattern
        assert slugify("Café") == "caf"
        assert slugify("Naïve") == "nave"
        assert slugify("München") == "mnchen"
        assert slugify("Hello 世界") == "hello"

    def test_long_strings(self):
        """Test handling of long strings."""
        long_text = "This is a very long text that should be properly slugified " * 3
        result = slugify(long_text)
        assert result.startswith
